emitted_text_v9: Earth is approximately an oblate spheroid.
Modern physics has useful descriptions of gravity, but no complete quantum theory is established.
Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into sugars and oxygen.
The nucleus contains most human cellular DNA.
Friction converts some motion into heat.
The two moons of Mars are Phobos and Deimos.
Scientific models describe observations and can be useful without being final truth.
Normal human body temperature is often given as about 37 degrees Celsius.
The capital of Italy is Rome.
